commit.message:
fix: swap mssql/server for azure-sql-edge on ARM64 hosts

The Azure Service Bus emulator depends on SQL Server, which has no ARM64
image. On Apple Silicon (and other ARM64 hosts), the mssql/server:2022-latest
container fails under QEMU with jemalloc address mapping errors.

Use Aspire's BeforeStartEvent to intercept the container model and swap the
image to azure-sql-edge:latest, which has native ARM64 support. The Service
Bus emulator works with either SQL engine.

commit.message:
Add MFI_UPX CMake option for automatic binary compression

New option: -DMFI_UPX=ON finds upx on PATH and runs 'upx --best' as a
post-build step on each executable. Compression flags are configurable
via MFI_UPX_FLAGS cache variable.

  cmake --preset mips-release -DMFI_UPX=ON
  cmake --build --preset mips-release

Results: mfi-mqtt-client 1.14MB → 451KB, mfi-rest-server 741KB → 286KB,
mfi-cli 692KB → 262KB. Default is OFF for faster iteration.

commit.message:
Replace dynamic_pointer_cast with static_pointer_cast, enable -fno-rtti

The two dynamic_pointer_cast calls in hass_mqtt_device (switch.cpp and
on_off_light.cpp) always downcast to the exact type registered in init(),
so the cast is guaranteed to succeed. static_pointer_cast is sufficient
and does not require RTTI.

This allows adding -fno-rtti to release builds, eliminating all typeinfo
and vtable name strings from the binary.

Size impact (mfi-mqtt-client):
  text: 1,137,620 → 1,120,440 (-17,180 bytes)
  data:    48,644 →    47,808 (-836 bytes)
  disk:      1.2M →      1.2M (692→692, modest)
  UPX:      452KB →     451KB

All 135 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Optimize release binary size and document UPX compression

Added compile/link flags for release builds:
- -fno-unwind-tables -fno-asynchronous-unwind-tables (reduce .eh_frame)
- -fmerge-all-constants (deduplicate constants)
- -fvisibility=hidden -fvisibility-inlines-hidden (reduce symbol exports)
- -Wl,--as-needed -Wl,--exclude-libs,ALL (trim unused library deps)

Updated README with accurate binary sizes and UPX compression guide.
UPX --best reduces on-disk size by ~62% (1.2MB → 452KB for mqtt-client)
with no runtime dependencies — binaries self-extract at startup.

commit.message:
BREAKING: Externalize all card/effect/encounter definitions to JSON configs (Roadmap Step 14) (#38)

Move 170+ hardcoded card definitions from 9 Rust discipline modules into
JSON configuration files under configurations/. Files are embedded at

New files:
- src/library/config.rs: JSON-deserializable config types (CardEntry tagged
  enum, DisciplineConfig, encounter config variants)
- src/library/config_loader.rs: Compile-time JSON embedding, name resolution
  (prefix:name effect references), and Library builder
- configurations/general/{tokens,shared_effects}.json: Token balances and
  shared card effects
- configurations/{combat,mining,herbalism,woodcutting,fishing,rest,crafting,
  research,milestone}/cards.json: Per-discipline card definitions

Key design decisions:
- Single ordered cards array per discipline (not three-pass) to preserve
  positional card IDs exactly matching original registration order
- Effect name references (e.g. 'shared:deal_damage', 'combat:enemy_damage')
  resolved to numeric IDs during loading
- Crafting cost calculation stays in Rust (computed by lib.add_card())
- Milestone tier-1 cards defined in JSON; tier-2+ generated at runtime

Removed ~3,900 lines of hardcoded registration code from discipline modules.
Added 69 new integration tests for coverage. 131 tests pass, 83% line coverage.

Updated designer.rs docs, README, and vision.md to reflect new config architecture.

commit.message:
Allow release CIs to run when a labeled PR receives new commits (#7741)

Release CIs only triggered on `labeled` events, requiring developers to
remove and re-add the "run release CIs" label after each commit push to
re-trigger builds.

### Changes

- Added `synchronize` to the `pull_request` trigger types in
`create_release.yml`

```yaml
pull_request:
  branches: [main, rel-*]
  types:
    - labeled
    - synchronize  # now re-triggers on new commits
```

Existing job-level conditions
(`contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'run release CIs')`)
already guard against running release jobs on unlabeled PRs, so this
change has no effect on PRs without the label.

feat: Research hidden-multiplier deduction gameplay (#36)

BREAKING: New deck-based Research experiment mechanic replaces simple
Insight payment. Players play 3 Research cards per round against 3
hidden symbol slots (ResearchSymbol: Alpha-Zeta). Position matches
yield 100, type-only matches yield 10. Insight costs escalate linearly
(round N costs N × 5). Player deduces hidden types from per_card_yield
feedback and decides when to stop.

New types: ResearchSymbol enum, ResearchRoundResult, ResearchDef fields
(target_size, position_match_yield, type_match_yield, base_insight_cost).

rounds_played, round_history, experiment_active.

New CardKind::Research variant and CardEffectKind::ResearchProbe variant.
26 Research player cards: 18 basic (single-symbol), 6 dual-symbol
premium (Stamina cost), 2 triple-symbol premium (Health cost).

New actions: ResearchPlayHand { card_ids }, ResearchConcludeExperiment.
Auto-begin experiment on first ResearchPlayHand.

commit.message:
Implement milestone encounters (Roadmap Step 13) (#34)

Add milestone encounter system — per-discipline tougher encounters that
reward 50%-improved CardEffects and offer escalating tier progression.

Key features:
- 5 discipline milestones: Combat, Mining, Herbalism, Woodcutting, Fishing
- Exponential insight cost: 100 * 2^(tier-1) MilestoneInsight
- Win → 50% better CardEffects + MilestoneScouting (pick 1 of 3 next-tier)
- Loss → reset encounter, return card to hand, back to NoEncounter
- Dedicated milestone hand separate from regular encounters (max 5)
- Abort treated as loss (reset + NoEncounter)

Types added: MilestoneDef, MilestoneEncounterState, EncounterPhase::MilestoneScouting,
  ActionPayload::MilestonePickScoutingChoice, TokenType::MilestoneMaxHand
New module: src/library/disciplines/milestone.rs (~900 lines)

removed from vision.md/roadmap.md, designer.rs and hints.rs updated.

TokenType enums all have new variants.

Rename SoRaytrace* API to generic SoScene*/SoSc* names (#389)

* Initial plan

* Rename SoRaytrace*/SoRt* API to SoScene*/SoSc* for generic rendering

The old names implied CPU raytracing exclusively; these classes are also
used by rasterization backends such as Vulkan.  Rename to reflect the
more generic purpose:

  SoRaytracingParams       -> SoSceneRendererParams
  SoRaytracerSceneCollector-> SoSceneCollector
  SoRtMaterial             -> SoScMaterial
  SoRtTriangle             -> SoScTriangle
  SoRtLightType            -> SoScLightType
  SoRtLightInfo            -> SoScLightInfo
  SoRtTextOverlay          -> SoScTextOverlay
  SO_RT_DIRECTIONAL        -> SO_SC_DIRECTIONAL
  SO_RT_POINT              -> SO_SC_POINT
  SO_RT_SPOT               -> SO_SC_SPOT

- Create new files (headers + sources) for all three classes with
  updated names, guards, doc comments, and description text.
- Update all call sites: SoActions.h, SoNodes.h, SoAction.cpp,
  SoNode.cpp, SoProceduralShape.cpp, all CMakeLists.txt files,
  all test utilities and rendering tests, and both doc pages.
- Delete the six superseded files.

commit.message:
Use attention instead of hard coding items/enemies/projectiles (#174)

* Unified entity attention: replace separate enemy/item/projectile arrays with transformer self-attention

Replace three separate fixed-size entity arrays (enemies 4×6, items 2×4, projectiles 2×5) with a unified 12-slot entity list processed by self-attention. Each entity gets a 9-dim feature vector (presence, position, direction, health, stun, hurts_on_touch, killable) plus a learned type embedding for ~74 entity types.

Architecture changes:
- EntityAttentionEncoder: type embedding (8-dim) + linear projection + 1-layer TransformerEncoder (4 heads, ff=128) + mean pooling
- CombinedExtractor: image CNN (256) + entity attention (64) + boolean info (15)
- Handles all-empty entity slots gracefully (unmask dummy token to prevent NaN softmax)

Observation changes:
- Unified entities (12×9) and entity_types (MultiDiscrete) replace enemy_features/item_features/projectile_features
- Clock item support: sets enemy hurts_on_touch=0, stun=1.0 when active
- Added has_clock boolean (index 14), kept full_health (index 13)

Also updates:
- Debugger observation panel: entity list widget replacing vector circle grid
- Rollout buffer: MultiDiscrete observation support
- All tests updated and passing (678 pass)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix empty action mask crash: safety fallback + rich diagnostics

When Link is stuck (can_link_move returns False for all directions) AND
no enemies/items are present (sword/beams disabled), the flat action mask
can end up all-False. In multihead mode this produces an all-False action
type mask, causing a crash in Categorical distribution.

Fix:
- action_space.py: if flat mask is entirely empty, force-enable all MOVE
  directions as a safety valve so the agent can always act
- models.py: replace bare assert with ValueError that includes full mask
  values, action type mask, direction mask, and action space config for
  easier debugging if this ever triggers again

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace safety fallback with diagnostic crash on empty action mask

Link should always have at least one valid move direction. An empty mask
indicates a bug in can_link_move or tile walkability, not a normal edge
case to paper over. The RuntimeError now dumps: location, position, tile,
link status, per-direction can_link_move results, actions_possible,
invalid_actions from training hints, enemy/item counts, and the full flat
mask. The models.py ValueError also includes the obs info vector.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add GAE checkbox to debugger toolbar (off by default)

The GAE advantage recomputation was running on every step, slowing down
the debugger. Add a 'GAE' checkbox to the toolbar that controls whether
advantages are calculated. When unchecked (default), the Advantage column
is empty and no computation happens. Toggling on recomputes immediately;
toggling off clears all advantage values. The γ/λ spinboxes still trigger
recomputation only when GAE is enabled.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(logics): update pyavd API compatibility for v6.x (#68)

* fix(generator): update pyavd API usage for compatibility with pyavd 6.x

pyavd 6.0.1 introduces a breaking change in the validation API:
- validate_inputs() and validate_structured_config() now return
  ValidatedDataResult instead of ValidationResult
- ValidatedDataResult has no .failed attribute; use .validated_data is None
- Violations are accessed via .validation_result.violations (not .validation_errors)
- Deprecations are accessed via .validation_result.deprecations

Also bump pyavd dependency from 5.7.2 to 6.0.1 and regenerate uv.lock.

Closes #67
Supersedes #59

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(logics): fix remaining pyavd 6.x API compatibility issues

- Replace removed 'name_servers' with 'dns_settings.servers' in example
  inventories (AVD 6.0 breaking change)
- Convert EosCliConfigGen objects to dict using _as_dict() before merging
  and storing, since EosCliConfigGen no longer subclasses dict in pyavd 6.x
- Replace removed private pyavd.api._anta.get_minimal_structured_configs()
  with public pyavd.api.anta.AVDFabricData.from_structured_configs() API
- Update get_device_test_catalog() call to use fabric_data= parameter

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(logics): broaden exception catch in ANTA catalog generation

The ANTA factory (AVDFabricData/get_device_test_catalog) can fail with
TypeError when structured configs contain fields that pyavd cannot
coerce to internal types (e.g. in test mocks that return plain dicts
with string platform values).

Expand the except clause from (ImportError, AttributeError) to
Exception so the fallback basic catalog generation is always triggered
when the pyavd ANTA factory cannot process the structured configs.

Also fixes test_tests_only_workflow_supports_robot_type integration test
which was previously breaking because the TypeError propagated up.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
